title: Bengali Alphabets – Bangla Barnamala (Eastern Nagari)
content: Bengali is written in the Eastern Nagari script. Learn vowels (স্বরবর্ণ) and consonants (ব্যঞ্জনবর্ণ).
intro: Bangla uses 11 vowel letters and about 39 consonants. Each consonant has an inherent vowel /ɔ/; vowel signs (কার) attach to consonants.

title: Vowels, Consonants in Bengali and their pronunciation
content: Pronunciation of Bengali vowels and consonants. Modern Bengali often does not distinguish short vs long ই/ঈ and উ/ঊ in speech.
intro: Each letter maps to fairly consistent sounds. Nasalization (ঁ, ং) is common. Practice with the speaker.

blocks.heading: Vowel pronunciation
blocks.content: Front vowels: ই, ঈ. Central: অ, আ. Back: উ, ঊ, ও, ও. Diphthongs: ঐ, ঔ. In Sanskrit loans, spelling may differ from colloquial pronunciation.

blocks.heading: Consonant pronunciation notes
rows: আপনি কি আমাকে সাহায্য করতে পারবেন?
Āpni ki āmākē sāhājjo kôrte pārben?

title: Bengali Barakhadi: vowel signs (কার) with consonants
content: How vowels combine with consonants in Bangla. The inherent vowel is ô; other vowels use kār (া, ি, ী, etc.).

blocks.heading: Matra examples
blocks.content: Same pattern applies to other consonants. হসন্ত (্) removes the inherent vowel for consonant clusters (যুক্তাক্ষর).

blocks.heading: Words and sentences using vowel signs
rows: How do you say ... in Bengali?

title: Frequently faced pronunciation problems in Bengali (Bangla script)
content: Common pitfalls: অ vs আ, ই/ঈ merge, স/শ/ষ, ন/ণ, ড/দ.
intro: Learners familiar with Hindi may notice differences in vowel quality and conjuncts.

blocks.heading: Tips
blocks.content: Aspiration matters: ক vs খ, গ vs ঘ. Retroflex ট ঠ ড ঢ differs from dental ত থ দ ধ.

blocks.heading: Practice sentences
rows: Is there someone who speaks English?

title: Pronunciation of Anusvara (ং) and related symbols in Bengali
content: ং (anusvara) nasalizes or represents a nasal consonant. ঃ (visarga) is rare in Bengali. ঁ (chandrabindu) nasalizes vowels.
intro: অনুস্বার (ং) often sounds like ঙ or ন depending on the following consonant.

title: Combining consonants in Bengali (যুক্তাক্ষর)
content: Consonant clusters use হসন্ত (্) to join letters: ক্ + ত = ক্ত (kto).
intro: যুক্তাক্ষর is common in Sanskrit and English loanwords.

blocks.heading: Sentences using consonant combinations
rows: আমি কি আপনার ফোন ব্যবহার করতে পারি?
Āmi ki āpār phōn bôbohār kôrte pāri?
I want to talk to a lawyer
আমি একজন আইনজীবীর সঙ্গে কথা বলতে চাই
Āmi ekjon āinjībīr songe kōthā bolte chāi

title: Combining consonants with র (r) – Bengali যুক্তাক্ষর Part 2
content: র can appear as রেফ (reph) before a consonant or in clusters like প্র, ক্র, স্ত.
intro: Practice reading words with র in the middle or end.

title: Combining consonants with হ (h) – Bengali যুক্তাক্ষর Part 3
content: হ combines as in ক্ষ (kh), ম্হ (mha), or in ঠ + হ.
intro: হ is common in Bengali grammar words.

title: Special or separate symbols for combined consonants in Bengali
content: Some ligatures have special shapes: ক্ষ, জ্ঞ, দ্র, etc.
intro: These appear in many Sanskrit and formal words.

title: Pronouns and Articles in Bengali
content: Bengali pronouns: আমি (I), তুমি (you informal), আপনি (you formal), সে (he/she), এ (this), ও (that).
intro: No definite article; demonstratives এ/ও/সে often do article-like work.

title: Using plurals to indicate respect in Bengali
content: Formal address often uses plural verb forms with আপনি or honorific third person (তিনি).
intro: তিনি যান (he/she goes - honorific) vs সে যায় (neutral).

title: Verbs in Bengali
content: Verbs take endings for tense and person: করি, করো, করে, করেন (present of 'do').
intro: Learn common roots: করা, যাওয়া, খাওয়া, দেখা, বলা.

title: Simple Present Tense in Bengali
content: Present habitual: আমি করি, তুমি করো, সে করে, আপনি করেন.
intro: Use করি for I, করো for informal you, করেন for formal you/he/she honorific.

title: Simple Present Tense of "To Be" in Bengali
content: Copula: আছি (I am), আছো (you inf.), আছে (he/she), আছেন (formal).
intro: Often omitted in equational sentences: সে ছাত্র (he student).

title: Present Continuous Tense in Bengali
content: Structure: আমি করছি (I am doing), তুমি করছো, সে করছে.
intro: Also: যাচ্ছি (I am going) from যাওয়া.

title: Simple Future Tense in Bengali
content: Future: করব (I will do), করবে (he/she), করবেন (formal).
intro: Also চলে যাবে (will go) patterns.

title: Future Continuous Tense in Bengali
content: Less common; often use future + সময় (time) or করতে থাকব.
intro: Colloquial: যাচ্ছি আবার (I will keep going).

title: Simple Past Tense in Bengali – Part 1 (Verbs without object)
content: Past: গেলাম (I went), গেলি (you inf.), গেল (he/she), গেলেন (formal).
intro: Intransitive past uses গেলাম pattern for যাওয়া.

title: Simple Past Tense in Bengali – Part 2 (Verbs with object)
content: Transitive past often uses করেছিলাম / করলাম: আমি বইটি পড়লাম.
intro: Perfect style: আমি খেয়েছি (I have eaten).

title: Simple Past Tense in Bengali – Part 3 (Past tense of "to be")
content: Was/were: ছিলাম, ছিলি, ছিল, ছিলেন.
intro: আমি ছিলাম (I was), সে ছিল (he/she was).

title: Exceptional Verbs in Bengali which change in past tense form
content: Some verbs have suppletive or irregular past: যাওয়া→গেল, আসা→এল, দেওয়া→দিল.
intro: Memorize common pairs.

title: Past Continuous Tense in Bengali
content: ছিল + করছিলাম pattern: আমি করছিলাম (I was doing).
intro: Common in narrative.

title: Present/Past/Future Perfect Tense in Bengali
content: Use করেছি / করেছিলাম / করব with auxiliary nuances; often same as English perfect with context.
intro: Colloquial: খেয়েছি (I have eaten).

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: By then I will have gone

title: Learn Prepositions in Bengali
content: Common: এ (in), থেকে (from), সঙ্গে (with), উপরে (on), নিচে (under).
intro: Postpositions often follow noun: বাড়িতে (at home).

title: Preposition "TO" in Bengali
content: Goal/direction: এ (to place), কাছে (towards someone), এর দিকে (towards).
intro: যাওয়া takes destination in dative/locative style.

title: Sentences with person or living things as object in Bengali
content: Animate objects take কে: আমি তাকে দেখি (I see him).
intro: Use করে for respect where needed.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: আমি তোমার ভাইয়ের সাথে দেখা করলাম
Āmi tōmār bhāiēr sāthe dēkhā korlām

title: Saying My/His/Her in Bengali
content: Possessives: আমার, তোমার, তার, আপনার, আমাদের.
intro: Pronoun + noun: আমার বই (my book).

title: Basic questions and "WH" questions in Bengali
content: কী, কে, কোথায়, কখন, কেন, কীভাবে.
intro: Question word often first: তুমি কোথায় যাও?

title: Negative Sentences – Present tense in Bengali
content: না / নই / নেই: আমি যাই না (I don't go), নেই = there isn't.
intro: নই for copula negation in some contexts.

title: Negative Sentences – Past tense in Bengali
content: না / হয়নি: আমি যাইনি (I did not go).
intro: খাইনি (I did not eat).

title: Negative Sentence – Future Tense in Bengali
content: Future negation: করব না, যাব না, হবে না.
intro: আমি আসব না (I will not come).

title: Working with nouns – Gender & Plurals in Bengali
content: Many nouns are unmarked for gender; humans and some animals use ছেলে/মেয়ে; plural often -রা or -গুলো.
intro: ছেলেরা খেলছে (the boys are playing); গরু (cow) has no natural gender suffix in the noun.

title: Working with nouns – Prepositions in Bengali
content: Postpositions follow the noun: বাড়িতে (at home), টেবিলের উপরে (on the table).
intro: Use এ/য়ে/te for locative and genitive chains as in textbooks.

blocks.heading: Nouns + postpositions
rows: The book is on the table
The cat is under the chair
The keys are in the bag
Wait in front of the gate
The shop is across the road

title: Prepositions with similar pronunciation or similar meanings in Bengali
content: কাছে (near) vs পাশে (beside); উপরে (on top) vs ওপরে (above).
intro: Listen for minimal pairs in context.

blocks.heading: Near / beside / on / above
rows: The lamp is on the roof
The dog is under the table

title: Cases in Bengali (কারক)
content: Sanskrit-style case labels: কর্তা (subject), কর্ম (object), করণ (instrument), etc.
intro: In spoken Bangla, postpositions often carry case-like meaning.

title: Commands / Imperative statements in Bengali
content: Polite imperatives use -ুন; informal use -ো/ো: বসুন, বসো, যাও.
intro: Negative: করবেন না / করো না.

title: Time related words in Bengali
content: আজ, কাল, গতকাল, পরশু; সকাল, দুপুর, বিকেল, রাত.
intro: Use করি / করব with time adverbs for habits and plans.

blocks.heading: Time words
rows: See you the day after tomorrow

title: Verbs indicating cause of action (Causative verb) in Bengali
content: Causative: খাইয়ে দেওয়া (feed / make eat), পড়ানো (teach / make read).
intro: Pattern: causative stem + দেওয়া / দেয়া in many patterns.

blocks.heading: Causative
rows: He opened the door for me

title: Gender-number agreement rule in Bengali
content: Verbs often agree with honorific level; plural subjects may take plural agreement in some styles.
intro: ছেলেরা খেলছে (they play) — plural subject + collective verb form.

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Tense – Second Style in Bengali
content: Colloquial perfect: করেছি, করেছিলাম; future perfect style: করে ফেলব, করে নেব.
intro: Often matches English perfect with 'already' or 'by then'.

blocks.heading: Perfect (style 2)
rows: By then I will have finished
By Monday I will have done it

title: Past/Present/Future Perfect Continuous tense in Bengali
content: Use করছিল + থাকা / করতে থাকা patterns: long duration up to a point.
intro: I had been reading / I have been working — often করছিলাম / করছিল + context.

blocks.heading: Perfect continuous
rows: By then she will have been teaching

title: Difference between "no/not" and "don't want" in Bengali
content: না negates verbs; চাই না = don't want; নেই = there isn't; নই = am not (copula).
intro: আমি যাই না (I don't go) vs আমি চাই না (I don't want it).

title: Sentence using "Going to" phrase in Bengali
content: Future intent uses infinitive + যাচ্ছি/যাচ্ছে: খেতে যাচ্ছি (I am going to eat).
intro: Same pattern for planned actions about to happen.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I am going to buy a book.
I am going to call you.
We are not going to wait.
When are you going to start?

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase in Bengali
content: Habitual past: …তাম / …তেন with time adverbs (আগে, প্রায়).
intro: তুমি খেলতে (you used to play) — informal past habitual.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: He used to read a lot.
We used to meet every week.
We used to play in this field.
I did not use to smoke.
They used to live in Dhaka.
I used to wake up early.
We used to take the bus.
Did you use to know him?

title: Sentence using "Used to" phrase – old style in Bengali
content: Older/literary habit: …ইত / …ত / ছিল before verb stem in some texts.
intro: Colloquial modern Bangla prefers …তাম; this lesson shows older-flavour lines.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: We used to study under the tree.
He used to ride a bicycle.
They used to celebrate the fair.
I used to fear the dark.
We used to listen to the radio.
He used to farm this land.
I used to visit every month.
We used to sleep on the roof.
They used to cross the river by boat.
Time used to pass slowly then.

title: Sentence using "If-Then" in Bengali
content: Condition: যদি … (তাহলে) … — তাহলে may be omitted in speech.
intro: First conditional style: present/future in result clause.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: If it rains, we will stay.
If he agrees, we will start.
If you are late, I will leave.
তুমি দেরি করলে আমি চলে যাব।
Tumi dēri korle āmi chōlē jābo.
If there is time, we will go.
If you study, you will pass.
If it is hot, drink water.
If I find it, I will return it.
Unless you try, you will not learn.
If the shop is closed, come back.
If the train is late, wait.
If winter comes, we will buy a coat.
If it is true, I will believe.
If they agree, the deal is done.

title: Prepositions with Verbs in Bengali
content: Many verbs take fixed postpositions: নির্ভর করা (on), অপেক্ষা করা (for), বিশ্বাস করা (in).
intro: The postposition follows the object or is frozen in the phrase.

title: Adjectives in Bengali
content: Adjectives usually precede the noun: বড় বাড়ি, সুন্দর ফুল.
intro: Reduplication can intensify: ধীরে ধীরে, গরম গরম.

title: Using verb "Can/To be able to" in Bengali
content: Ability: পারা + infinitive — আমি পড়তে পারি (I can read).
intro: Negative: পারি না.

title: Using verb To Want/To Need in Bengali
content: Want: চাই + object or infinitive; need: দরকার / লাগে.
intro: আমার জল চাই; আমার বিশ্রাম দরকার.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: সে তোমার সঙ্গে দেখা করতে চায়।
Sē tōmār songe dēkhā korte chāy.

title: Verb "To Become/To Happen" in Bengali
content: হওয়া covers 'become' and 'happen': অন্ধকার হয়ে গেল, কী হয়েছে?
intro: Also হয়ে যাওয়া for change of state.

title: Using "Should" in Bengali
content: Obligation/advice: উচিত, ভালো হয়, করা উচিত.
intro: আপনি বিশ্রাম নেওয়া উচিত — formal advice.

title: Using "Must"/"to be have to" in Bengali
content: Strong obligation: করতে হবে, যেতেই হবে, বাধ্য.
intro: Formal: আপনাকে … করতে হবে.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: তাকে প্রধানশিক্ষকের সঙ্গে দেখা করতে হবে।
Tākē prôdhānshikkhokēr songe dēkhā korte hōbe.
I have to wake up early.
I have to call my mother.

title: Using phrase "to keep doing" in Bengali
content: Continuative: করতে করতে, চলতে থাকা, করতে থাকা.
intro: বৃষ্টি হতে হতে চলছিল — while it kept raining.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: তারা অপেক্ষা করতে করতে বসে ছিল।
Tārā ôpekhshā korte korte bôse chhil.
আমি পড়তে পড়তে রাত হয়ে গেল।
Āmi pōṛte pōṛte rāt hōye gēl.
He kept looking at the clock.
সে ঘড়ি দেখতে দেখতে বসে ছিল।
Sē ghoṛi dekhte dekhte bôse chhil.
আমরা আশা করতে করতে অপেক্ষা করলাম।
Āmrā āshā korte korte ôpekhshā kollām.
তারা তর্ক করতে করতে উঠে গেল।
Tārā tôrkô korte korte uṭhe gēl.
আমি অনুশীলন করতে করতে ভালো হলাম।
Āmi ônushīlon korte korte bhālō hlām.

title: Comparison and degrees of comparison in Bengali
content: More than: চেয়ে বেশি; most: সবচেয়ে; less: কম.
intro: এটা ওটার চেয়ে বড়।

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: She is as tall as her brother.

title: Using verb "To Know" in Bengali
content: Know: জানা, চেনা (person); know how: জানতে পারা.
intro: আমি জানি না — I don't know.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: কাল কি হবে কেউ জানে না।
Kāl ki hōbe keu jānē nā.

title: Using "Let" and "Shall we" in Bengali
content: Suggestion: চলো … যাই, … করি; permission: let him = তাকে … করতে দিন.
intro: আমরা কি … করব? — Shall we?

title: Which-That / What-That sentences in Bengali
content: Relative clauses: যে, যা, যেটা — যে বইটা কিনেছি.
intro: The book that I bought — বইটা যে কিনেছি.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: The book that I bought is here.
The man who called is my uncle.
যে লোকটা ফোন করেছিল সে আমার কাকা।
Jē lōkṭā phōn korechil sē āmār kākā.
This is the house that we built.
এটা সেই বাড়ি যেটা আমরা বানিয়েছি।
Ēṭā sei bāṛi jēṭā āmrā bāniyechhi.
I remember the day when we met.
আমি আমাদের দেখা হওয়ার দিন মনে রাখি।
Āmi āmādēr dēkhā hōẏār din mōne rākhi.
Show me which one you like.
The news that I heard is true.
I do not know who did it.
The place where we met is closed.
That depends on what you do.
সেটা তোমার উপর নির্ভর করে যা করবে।
Sēṭā tōmār upor nirbhor kōre jā korbe.
I have the pen that you gave.
আমার কলম আছে যেটা তুমি দিয়েছিলে।
Āmār kolom āchhe jēṭā tumi diẏechhile.

title: Giving instructions formally in Bengali
content: Polite imperatives: করুন, দিন, যান; দয়া করে …
intro: Used with strangers and elders.

title: Using "To Like" in Bengali
content: Like: পছন্দ করো, ভালো লাগে; noun: আমার পছন্দ.
intro: আমার গান পছন্দ — I like songs.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I like walking in the park.
I like this more than that.
আমার এটা ওটার চেয়ে বেশি ভালো লাগে।
Āmār ēṭā ōṭār cheye bēshi bhālō lāgē.
তুমি কি আমার উপহার পছন্দ করো?
Tumi ki āmār upohār pôchondo kōrō?

title: Sentences using "would" in Bengali
content: Habitual past or polite: করতাম, হত, করতেন; conditional would: করতাম.
intro: আমি প্রতি সপ্তাহে যেতাম — I would go every week.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: We would meet at the park.

title: "To Understand" & "To come to know" in Bengali
content: Understand: বুঝতে পারা; come to know: জানতে পারা, টের পাওয়া.
intro: হঠাৎ জানতে পারলাম — I suddenly came to know.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I know now why he left.
এখন বুঝি কেন সে চলে গেছে।
Ēkhon bujhi kēn sē chōlē gēchhe.
She did not understand at first.
I came to know the news.

title: Add a question tag in Bengali
content: Tags: তো, না, তাই না — তুমি আসছো, তো?
intro: Softens statements into questions.

title: Using verb "To remember" in Bengali
content: Remember: মনে রাখা; forget: ভুলে যাওয়া.
intro: মনে করিয়ে দিও — remind me.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: আমি তার নাম মনে করতে পারছি না।
Āmi tār nām mōne korte pārchhi nā.
She reminded me of the time.

title: Using "To Want"/"To Need" with other verbs in Bengali
content: Chain: চাইতে চাই, যেতে চাই, করতে চাই না.
intro: আমি শিখতে চাই — I want to learn.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: He wants to buy a car.
I do not want to argue.
সে তার সঙ্গে দেখা করতে চায়।
Sē tār songe dēkhā korte chāy.
I want to read this book.
He needs to see a doctor.

title: Using "To Want" with other person in Bengali
content: Desire for another's action: চাই যে সে …, আমি চাই তুমি …
intro: আমি চাই তুমি পড়ো — I want you to read.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I want you to read this.
I want the boy to study.
I do not want him to smoke.
আমি চাই না সে ধূমপান করুক।
Āmi chāi nā sē dhūmpān koruk.
I want everyone to be safe.
I want you to be happy.

title: Using phrase "supposed to" in Bengali
content: Plan or expectation: কথা ছিল, হওয়ার কথা ছিল, হওয়ার কথা.
intro: আমাদের দেখা হওয়ার কথা ছিল — we were supposed to meet.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: The train was due at five.
I was supposed to leave early.
The shop was supposed to open.
The meeting was supposed to start.
I was supposed to bring food.
The bus was supposed to be here.
The exam was supposed to be easy.
The guest was supposed to stay.
The train was supposed to be late.
ট্রেন দেরি করার কথা ছিল না।
Ṭrēn dēri korār kōthā chhil nā.
The letter was supposed to arrive.

title: Using "To Like" with other verbs in Bengali
content: Pattern: … গাইতে ভালো লাগে, … করে পছন্দ.
intro: আমার গান গাইতে ভালো লাগে — I like to sing.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: সে ভাত রান্না করতে পছন্দ করে।
Sē bhāt rānnā korte pôchondo kōre.
We like walking in the park.
আমার মানুষকে সাহায্য করতে ভালো লাগে।
Āmār mānuṣke sāhājjo korte bhālō lāgē.
We like to travel by train.
আমাদের ট্রেনে ভ্রমণ করতে ভালো লাগে।
Āmādēr ṭrēne bhromaṇ korte bhālō lāgē.
সে গাড়ি ঠিক করতে পছন্দ করে।
Sē gāṛi ṭhik korte pôchondo kōre.

title: Verbs which use preposition "To" with subject and verb form as per object in Bengali
content: Many verbs take লাগে: আমার লাগে (I need), চাইতে লাগে (begins to want).
intro: Animate object often takes কে: তাকে দেখা.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: The work is hard for me.
I want to talk to him.
আমি আমার কথা তাকে বলতে চাই।
Āmi āmār kōthā tākē bolte chāi.
I am looking for my keys.

title: Short forms in spoken Bengali
content: Colloquial reductions: কিরে, কী করছিস, এসো, না.
intro: Informal; use carefully with friends.

title: Greeting, Wishes, Blessings, Slogans in Bengali
content: নমস্কার, সালাম, শুভ সকাল; শুভেচ্ছা, ভালো থেকো.
intro: দিন শুভ হোক — may your day be good.

title: Compound verbs / Phrases in Bengali
content: Light verbs: দেওয়া, নেওয়া, ফেলা, দেওয়া — করে দেওয়া, খেয়ে নেওয়া.
intro: খেয়ে নিলাম — ate (completed).

title: Calling or Addressing someone in Bengali
content: Titles: স্যার, ম্যাডাম, দাদা, দিদি; respectful আপনি.
intro: ঠাকুরমা — grandmother (respectful).

title: Exclamations in Bengali
content: Surprise, pain, joy: ওহ্, আহ্, বাহ্, হায়.
intro: কী সুন্দর! — how beautiful!

title: Requesting someone – "May I", "Can you" etc. in Bengali
content: Polite request: কি … করতে পারি, একটু … করবেন, দয়া করে.
intro: আমি কি বসতে পারি? — may I sit?

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: আমি কি আপনার ফোন ব্যবহার করতে পারি?
Āmi ki āpnār phōn bẽbohār korte pāri?
আমি কি একটা প্রশ্ন করতে পারি?
Āmi ki ekṭā prôshno korte pāri?
আমি কি আপনার কলম নিতে পারি?
Āmi ki āpnār kolom nite pāri?
দয়া করে এক গ্লাস জল দিন।
Dôya kōre ek glās jol din.
আমি কি এখন চলে যেতে পারি?
Āmi ki ēkhon chōlē jēte pāri?
Can you tell me the time?

title: Showing uncertainty using May, Might in Bengali
content: Possibility: হতে পারে, সম্ভবত, হতেও পারে.
intro: বৃষ্টি হতে পারে — it may rain.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: সে আজ রাতে ফোন করতে পারে।
Sē āj rāte phōn korte pārē.

title: Adjectives made from verb in Bengali
content: Participial adjectives: করা, -ন্ত, -িত — পড়া বই, ভাঙা কাচ.
intro: A broken glass — ভাঙা কাচ.

title: Adjectives related to prepositions in Bengali
content: Locative/relational: ভিতরের, বাইরের, উপরের, নিচের.
intro: বাড়ির সামনের রাস্তা — the road in front of the house.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: The shop next to the bank.

title: Using May to express wish in Bengali
content: Wish: হোক, থাকুক, পাক — ভালো থেকো.
intro: তুমি সুস্থ হও — may you be well.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: May the new year be good.

title: Phrase "To Make Someone do something" in Bengali
content: Causative: করানো, খাওয়ানো, পড়ানো — তাকে দৌড়ানো.
intro: মা আমাকে দুধ খাওয়াল — mother made me drink milk.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: আমরা তাদের চলে যেতে বাধ্য করলাম।
Āmrā tādēr chōlē jēte bādhho korlām.

title: Generalizing words – somewhere, somehow, anything, anybody etc. in Bengali
content: Indefinites: কোথাও, কোনোভাবে, কিছু, কেউ, যে কেউ.
intro: কিছু একটা — something.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I will find a way somehow.
আমি কোনো না কোনোভাবে পথ পাব।
Āmi kōnō nā kōnobhābē poth pābō.

title: "To have" to indicate possession and relation in Bengali
content: Possession: আছে, আমার আছে; relation: আমার মা, তার ভাই.
intro: আমার দুটো বই আছে — I have two books.

title: Using "To mean". How to ask meaning in Bengali
content: Mean: মানে; ask: … এর মানে কী?, এটার অর্থ কী?
intro: এর মানে কী? — what does this mean?

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: What is the meaning of life?
আমি বুঝি তুমি কী বলতে চাইছ।
Āmi bujhi tumi ki bolte chāichchho.
Do not take it the wrong way.
What is the Bengali for this?

title: Conjunctions in Bengali – Part 1 (And, But, Because, So, That, Although, Though, Still, Yet)
content: এবং/আর, কিন্তু, কারণ, তাই, যে, যদিও, তবু, তথাপি.
intro: বৃষ্টি হচ্ছে, তবু যাব — it is raining, yet I will go.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: আমি এলাম কিন্তু সে চলে গেল।
Āmi ēlām kintu sē chōlē gēl.
I was tired, so I slept.
I know that you are busy.
Although it was late, we went.
We left early yet missed the train.
আমরা তাড়াতাড়ি বেরিয়েছি, তবু ট্রেন মিস করেছি।
Āmrā ṭāṛāṭāṛi beriẏechhi, tabu ṭrēn mis korechhi.
I wanted to go but I could not.
তাই আমরা অপেক্ষা করার সিদ্ধান্ত নিলাম।
Tāi āmrā ôpekhshā korār siddhānto nilām.
Although she was ill, she worked.
যদিও সে অসুস্থ, তবু কাজ করেছে।
Jôdio sē ôsustho, tabu kāj korechhe.
I said that I would come.
The food was cold but tasty.
He is tired; still he runs.
We study because we want to learn.

title: Conjunctions in Bengali – Part 2 (As, Or, Neither, Nor, If, in case, provided)
content: যেমন, অথবা, না … না, যদি, যদি … হয়, শর্ত সাপেক্ষে.
intro: চা না কফি? — tea or coffee?

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: If it rains, we will stay.
Take an umbrella in case it rains.
You may go provided you finish.
Neither rich nor poor, just human.
না ধনী না গরিব, শুধু মানুষ।
Nā dhonī nā gôrib, shudhu mānuṣ.
Call me if you need help.
না এটা না ওটা চলে না।
Nā ēṭā nā ōṭā chōlē nā.
If you hurry, you will catch it.
You can sit here or there.
তুমি এখানে বা ওখানে বসতে পারো।
Tumi ēkhāne bā ōkhāne boste pārō.

title: Conjunctions in Bengali – Part 3 (After, then, As far as, As long as, As if)
content: পরে, তারপর, যতদূর, যতক্ষণ, যেন.
intro: কাজ শেষ করে তারপর যাব — after finishing work, then I will go.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: দুপুরের খাওয়ার পরে আমরা বিশ্রাম নিলাম।
Dupurēr khāoẏār pōrē āmrā bishrām nilām.
As far as I know, he is fine.
Stay as long as you like.
He talks as if he knows everything.
সে এমন কথা বলে যেন সব জানে।
Sē ēmon kōthā bōlē jēn sôb jānē.
As far as the eye can see.
Wait as long as it takes.
She smiled as if she understood.
এটা শেষ করো, তারপর ওটা শুরু করো।
Ēṭā shēsh kōrō, tārpor ōṭā shuru kōrō.
As far as I am concerned, it is fine.
Sing as long as you can.
He looked as if he was lost.
সে এমন দেখাল যেন হারিয়ে গেছে।
Sē ēmon dēkhāl jēn hāriẏē gēchhe.
As long as we are together.

title: Conjunctions in Bengali – Part 4 (As Soon as, Once, Rather Than, Instead of, So that)
content: যেই না, একবার, বরং, এর বদলে, যেন.
intro: পড়া শেষ করলেই যাব — as soon as I finish reading, I will go.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: As soon as I saw him, I smiled.
Once you start, do not stop.
Speak clearly so that all understand.
As soon as the train stopped, we ran.
Once I know, I will tell.
Rather than wait, leave a message.
Instead of walking, take a rickshaw.
Close the door so that the cat does not go out.
দরজা বন্ধ করো যেন বেরাল বেরোয় না।
Dôrjā bondho kōrō jēn bērāl berōẏ nā.
As soon as it dawned, we left.
Once you try, you will like it.
Instead of the bus, take the metro.
Write slowly so that it is neat.
As soon as she called, I answered.
Once winter comes, we will travel.

title: List of Conjunctions in Bengali
content: Common linkers: এবং, কিন্তু, কারণ, তাই, যদি, যখন, যেন, অথবা.
intro: Mix these to build longer sentences.

title: Combining sentences in Bengali
content: Join with comma + conjunction or relative clause.
intro: সে এসেছে, এবং আমরা খেলেছি — he came, and we played.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I was tired, so I slept.
She sings and he plays tabla.
সে গায় আর সে তবলা বাজায়।
Sē gāy ār sē tablā bājāẏ.
It rained but we went out.
I know that he is honest.
If you are free, call me.
The book that I bought is good.
She opened the door and smiled.
We waited because the bus was late.
আমরা অপেক্ষা করেছি কারণ বাস দেরি করেছে।
Āmrā ôpekhshā korechhi kāroṇ bās dēri korechhe.
He ran fast and caught the train.
I like tea while she likes coffee.
They left early yet arrived late.
তারা তাড়াতাড়ি বেরিয়েছে, তবু দেরি করে পৌঁছেছে।
Tārā ṭāṛāṭāṛi beriẏechhe, tabu dēri kōre poũchhechhe.
Study hard so that you pass.
As soon as I heard, I came.
I will come if you invite me.
The sun rose and birds sang.

title: Compound verbs in Bengali
content: দেওয়া/নেওয়া/ফেলা + main verb for aspect.
intro: দেখে নিন — please look (and check).

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: I forgot to bring the file.

title: Active Passive voice in Bengali
content: Passive often: … করা হয়, … করা হল; agent: দ্বারা.
intro: রুটি কাটা হয় — bread is cut.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: The book was written by him.
The match will be played tomorrow.
The house was built last year.
The song was sung by her.
The work was finished on time.

title: Active Passive voice in Bengali – Style 2
content: Alternative: … যায়, … হতে পারে; impersonal passives.
intro: এটা করা যায় — this can be done.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: It is said that he left.

title: Using "To Feel" & "To think" in Bengali
content: Feel: লাগা, অনুভব করা; think: মনে করা, ভাবা.
intro: আমার ভালো লাগছে — I feel good.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: সে মনে করে আমাদের অপেক্ষা করা উচিত।
Sē mōne kōre āmādēr ôpekhshā korā uchit.
তারা মনে করে সে মিথ্যা বলছে।
Tārā mōne kōre sē mithyā bolchchhe.
We think the plan is good.

title: Idioms and Phrases in Bengali
content: Fixed expressions: হাতে হাতে, চোখে চোখ, মাথায় হাত.
intro: আকাশ কুসুম — castle in the air.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: All is well that ends well.
The pot calling the kettle black.
Every cloud has a silver lining.
Do not cry over spilt milk.
Time and tide wait for none.

title: Idioms and Phrases in Bengali – Part 2
content: More sayings: নাকে তেল দেওয়া, চোখ বুজে বিশ্বাস.
intro: মাছের তেলে মাছ — fish in troubled water (contextual).

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: To cost an arm and a leg.
To let the cat out of the bag.
To hit the nail on the head.
The ball is in your court.

title: Sentences using "If-Then" Style 2 in Bengali
content: Varied conditionals: থাকলে, হলে, না হলে, হলেই.
intro: সময় থাকলে আসব — if there is time, I will come.

blocks.heading: Examples
rows: If there is time, I will come.
If you call, I will answer.
If it does not rain, we will go.
If you hurry, you will catch it.
If I knew, I would tell.
If you were here, I would be happy.
If he had come, we would have left.
Unless you try, you will not learn.
If the bus is late, take a taxi.
If it is cheap, buy two.
If she agrees, we will start.
If there is a problem, call me.
If I had money, I would travel.
If you see him, give this.
If the shop is closed, come back.
If everything is fine, we will celebrate.

title: List of Body parts in Bengali
content: Common nouns for parts of the body; some have everyday compounds (e.g. চোখের পাতা).
intro: মাথা, চোখ, হাত — basic anatomy words.

title: Frequently used words – Miscellaneous in Bengali
content: High-frequency nouns for daily life, places, and objects.
intro: Use these in short sentences: আমার বাড়ি, আজ কাজ.

title: Relations in Bengali
content: Kinship terms; some vary by side (মামা vs কাকা).
intro: বাবা/মা for parents; দাদা/দিদি often for older siblings in the family.

title: Numbers in Bengali – Part 1
content: Cardinals ০–১০ and key tens; digits match Bangla numerals in writing.
intro: এক দুই তিন — practice counting aloud.

title: Numbers in Bengali – Part 2
content: Larger amounts, ordinals, and common number words in speech.
intro: হাজার, লাখ — used in prices and populations.

title: Fractional numbers, sequence, percentage in Bengali
content: Fractions, order words, and how people say percent in Bangla.
intro: ৫০ শতাংশ — fifty percent.

title: Frequently used verbs in Bengali – Part 1
content: Core action verbs; infinitives often end in –া / –তে.
intro: খাওয়া, যাওয়া — build sentences with আমি … করি.

title: Frequently used verbs in Bengali – Part 2
content: Verbs for wants, social actions, and daily errands.
intro: চাওয়া, কেনা — very common in shops and requests.

title: Frequently used verbs in Bengali – Part 3
content: Verbs for study, work, play, and household activities.
intro: রান্না করা, পরিষ্কার করা — daily routine.

title: Frequently used verbs in Bengali – Part 4
content: Verbs for placement, change, and finishing actions.
intro: রাখা, শেষ করা — narrative and instructions.

title: One verb, multiple meanings – দেওয়া / দেয়া in Bengali
content: দেওয়া often means “give”, but appears in many fixed expressions (দাম দেওয়া, ছেড়ে দেওয়া).
intro: Each row: English cue → natural Bangla phrase.

title: One verb, multiple meanings – লাগা in Bengali
content: লাগা covers “stick”, “feel”, “cost”, “need”, and more in compound verbs.
intro: ঠান্ডা লাগছে = I feel cold; দাম লাগে = it costs.

title: List of Bengali Adjectives – Part 1
content: Common adjectives for size, quality, temperature, and feeling.
intro: বড়/ছোট, ভালো/খারাপ — place before or after noun depending on phrase.

title: Adverbs in Bengali – Part 1
content: Time and place adverbs; many double as question words.
intro: এখন, আজ, এখানে — anchor your sentences in time and space.

title: List of adverbs in Bengali
content: Manner, probability, and focus adverbs for richer sentences.
intro: দ্রুত, ধীরে — describe how something happens.

title: List of vegetable names in Bengali
content: Everyday vegetables in markets and kitchens.
intro: আলু, পেঁয়াজ — core cooking ingredients.

title: List of names of fruits in Bengali
content: Common fruits; some names are shared with colour words (কমলা).
intro: আম, কলা — widely eaten across Bengal.

title: List of names of birds in Bengali
content: Common birds in towns and countryside.
intro: কাক, কোকিল — familiar sounds in Bengal.

title: List of names of insects in Bengali
content: Common insects; several appear in idioms and daily complaints.
intro: মশা, পিঁপড়ে — household vocabulary.

title: List of colours in Bengali
content: Basic and common colour words; often used as adjectives before nouns.

title: Simple Bengali Sentences – Where/Place Related
content: Asking directions and describing locations.

rows: It is near the post office.

Get down at the next stop.
The toilet is on the first floor.
There is a good restaurant nearby.
Where can I get a taxi?

blocks.heading: Conversation
rows: What is the rate per night?
I would like to extend one night.

title: Asking address in Bengali

blocks.heading: Conversation
rows: The pin code is seven zero zero zero one.

Pin kōḍ sāt shūnyo shūnyo shūnyo ek.
How do I reach from the station?
Take a left from the crossing.
I will send you the location.
I live on the third floor.

title: I Love you in Bengali – Proposing someone
content: Romantic but respectful phrases; adjust তুমি/আপনি to context.
intro: ভালোবাসি — I love you (intimate).

blocks.heading: Conversation
rows: You mean a lot to me.
I want to spend life with you.
আমি তোমার সঙ্গে জীবন কাটাতে চাই।
Āmi tōmār songe jībon kāṭāte chāi.
আমি কি তোমার বাবা-মার সঙ্গে দেখা করতে পারি?
Āmi ki tōmār bābā-mār songe dēkhā korte pāri?
I will wait for your answer.
You are beautiful inside and out.

title: Simple Bengali Conversation – Interview
content: Job interviews: background, skills, salary, joining.
intro: অভিজ্ঞতা — experience; বেতন — salary.

blocks.heading: Conversation
rows: I have five years of experience.
I worked as a team lead.
আমি টিম লিড হিসেবে কাজ করেছি।
Āmi ṭim liḍ hisēbē kāj korechhi.
Why do you want this job?
I can join in two weeks.
আমি দু সপ্তাহে যোগ দিতে পারি।
Āmi du soptāhē jōg dite pāri.
Do you have any questions for us?
We will inform you by email.

rows: Please come at seven in the morning.

Please lock the door when you leave.
